Title: Cytosolic glutamine synthetase is important for photosynthetic efficiency and water use efficiency in potato as revealed by high-throughput sequencing QTL analysis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase catalytic C-terminal domain PF00120.19; GS1 cytosolic isoform
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic GS1 branch of glutamine synthesis linked to photorespiratory nitrogen cycling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control point/QTL-associated determinant; rate-limiting not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent fixation of ammonium to glutamate to form glutamine, supporting reassimilation of photorespiratory ammonium
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and prevents nitrogen loss that supports photosynthetic efficiency and WUE
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen assimilation and remobilization; supports photorespiratory N cycling and photosynthetic performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Favorable alleles are associated with higher leaf GS activity, higher photosynthetic efficiency, and increased photosynthetic and irrigation water use efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This cytosolic GS1 gene lies within the major chromosome 1 QTL shared by high- and low-WUE bulks. 2. It is the only GS candidate with amino-acid-changing SNPs that segregate with the WUE bulks. 3. High-WUE bulks show higher total leaf GS activity under conditions where pWUE differs significantly, especially elevated CO2. 4. The gene likely contributes to limiting N loss from photorespiration and thereby supports photosynthesis and WUE.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL-seq/bulked segregant high-throughput sequencing; SNP segregation analysis; leaf glutamine synthetase enzyme activity assay; comparative expression support from potato genome datasets
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaf; FPKM 5.19 in DM1-3 516 R44 and 2.29 in RH89-039-16; among cytosolic GS1 clade genes it is slightly higher expressed than PGSC0003DMG401009595
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf extracts assayed; implicated in conditions with elevated CO2 where GS activity and pWUE differences are strongest
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing/QTL-seq of parents and bulks mapped to potato reference genome DM v4.03; reference expression (FPKM) from potato genome project for DM1-3 516 R44 and RH89-039-16
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Well-watered and well-fertilized growth; contrasting temperature, humidity, light, and CO2; strongest functional differentiation observed at elevated CO2 (1000 ppm)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate allele for breeding potato lines with improved photosynthetic efficiency and WUE, particularly by selecting favorable GS1 haplotypes; could be stacked with other N-assimilation/photorespiration genes, but field validation under ambient CO2 and lower N is needed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Effect may be context dependent and most evident under elevated CO2 and high-input conditions; authors note QTL may partly reflect rapid growth rather than WUE per se
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Four alleles were inferred to segregate between low and high bulks; amino-acid-changing SNPs fall in catalytic C-terminal domain, including substitutions discussed as potentially affecting stability/activity; considered the strongest candidate among GS genes in the QTL

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase catalytic domain; GS1 cytosolic isoform
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Candidate QTL gene; key-control status not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent synthesis of glutamine from glutamate and ammonium
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and supports photorespiratory ammonium reassimilation linked to WUE
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen assimilation and photorespiratory N recycling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as a cytosolic GS candidate within the chromosome 1 WUE QTL and proposed to contribute to improved photosynthetic efficiency/WUE
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This full-length cytosolic GS1 gene co-localizes with the major chromosome 1 WUE QTL. 2. It is expressed in leaf in reference potato datasets. 3. Together with PGSC0003DMG403009595, it constitutes a substantial fraction of cytosolic GS1 expression and is considered a candidate underlying the QTL.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL-seq/bulked segregant sequencing; positional candidate analysis; reference expression support
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf expression detected; FPKM 2.12 in DM1-3 516 R44 and 1.48 in RH89-039-16
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-associated candidate inferred from WUE QTL and leaf GS activity context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing/QTL-seq; reference RNA expression from potato genome project
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Well-watered and well-fertilized conditions; variable CO2, temperature, humidity, and light during phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker for selection of favorable cytosolic GS1 haplotypes in breeding for improved WUE/photosynthetic performance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Specific causal allele not functionally resolved; contribution may be smaller or less directly supported than PGSC0003DMG403009595
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No amino-acid-changing SNP evidence was highlighted for this gene; retained as a candidate because it is a full-length GS1 gene in the QTL

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase family; putatively truncated GS1-like sequence
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Potentially related to glutamine synthesis, but likely nonfunctional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Located in the chromosome 1 QTL, but considered unlikely to be functional because the gene appears truncated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This GS-like gene is located in the same chromosome 1 QTL as the other GS genes. 2. The authors state it seems truncated and thus unlikely to be functional. 3. In RH89-039-16, transcripts were not detected, supporting reduced relevance as the causal gene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL-seq positional identification; comparative expression evidence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf FPKM 4.73 in DM1-3 516 R44 and 0 in RH89-039-16
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely cytosolic if functional; not established
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing/QTL-seq; reference RNA expression from potato genome project
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value because gene is likely truncated/nonfunctional; mainly useful to exclude from causal prioritization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is one of the three GS genes highlighted in the QTL, but evidence argues against it being the main functional determinant
